theme: Five rules for incentive trips that work

linkedin.text:
Most incentive trips fail for the same reason: they're built like a conference, not a reward.

After producing incentive travel across 120+ countries, we've learned the difference comes down to five rules:

1. Reward, don't schedule. Free time is the gift — not the filler between sessions.
2. One unforgettable hero moment beats five merely good ones. Spend the budget where memory lives.
3. Mix the org chart at dinner. Culture gets built where the VP and the new hire share a table.
4. Make every transfer invisible. Friction is the only thing guests ever remember as "bad."
5. Brief, don't manage. Your top performers are adults on holiday — treat them like it.

We build all five in from day one. From business to pleasure.

Want our full planning checklist? DM us "CHECKLIST" or book a call.

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #MICE

facebook.text:
There's a thin line between an incentive trip people love and one they just tolerate — and it's usually these 5 things.

1) Reward, don't schedule — free time is the gift, not the filler.
2) One unforgettable hero moment beats five good ones.
3) Mix the org chart at dinner — that's where culture is built.
4) Make every transfer invisible — friction is the only thing guests remember as "bad."
5) Brief, don't manage — your people are adults on holiday.

We build all five in from day one. From business to pleasure.

Which one does your company get wrong most often? Tell us below — or message us "CHECKLIST" for our full planning guide.

Photorealistic, 35mm film grain, no CGI, no text, no logos.
